description_long: This is a large, rectangular apartment building with a flat roof and a symmetrical facade. The front features multiple rows of balconies with railings, each corresponding to a separate apartment unit, and the building is elevated slightly above a green base that resembles a lawn or communal area. The rear side is characterized by vertical structural elements and lacks balconies, instead showing a more utilitarian design with visible support columns. The sides of the building are plain, with regularly spaced windows and minimal architectural detailing. The color palette consists mainly of light pink, white, and gray tones, giving the building a modern yet utilitarian appearance. The structure appears to be constructed primarily from concrete, glass, and metal, typical of mid-to-late 20th-century urban residential architecture.
description: A large, rectangular apartment building with multiple balconies on the front, flat roof, and a modern, utilitarian design in light pink, white, and gray tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing rows of balconies and a symmetrical facade with railings and windows.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the building's length, plain walls, and regularly spaced windows.
description_view_2: Rear view with vertical structural elements, no balconies, and a more utilitarian appearance.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the building's length and window arrangement.
attribute_window_arrangement: Windows are arranged in evenly spaced horizontal rows, aligned with each floor and grouped symmetrically across the facade.
